title: Pin<Box<ClientInner>> prevents UB from C storing pointers into movable Rust data
description: wh_Client_Init stores the address of the whClientContext (and comm_cfg) in wolfCrypt's global CryptoCb table and in the wolfHSM internal state. If Client were on the stack or in a Vec that could reallocate, those stored pointers would dangle the moment Rust moved the struct. The Pin<Box<ClientInner>> design makes the address stable for the Client's entire lifetime. A future contributor might suggest simplifying to a plain struct or using Box<Client> without Pin. Pin is warranted here because the C library is an intrusive user of the address — plain Box is not sufficient to prevent moves (you can still move out of a Box via *box_val = new_val or mem::replace on the pointer). The pattern (all C state in one heap allocation, filled after boxing, with the initialization comment explaining the two-phase approach) is exactly the correct Rust idiom for this situation.
